comment.body:
first thing you need is mental control.
very important.

it requires two things:
1) self-control (if you can't even keep a daily routine that you planned, you won't be successful in trading neither),
2) knowledge (you must be educated abotu what you are doing in order to be able to rule your emotions)

comment.body:
the problem is always with the emotions.
so, this is why we have to learn very well the dynamics.
bcs even if you see it, know it, understand it, but you get scared on a temporal drawdown in your PnL, you will panic, and chase the flow.
and you will always lag in that case, bcs you will always trade what you already see... so you will trade the past, not frontrunning the market and looking into the future

comment.body:
i say "temporal" this case, bcs
imagine, that last gap down we had. 
I said: buy, it is bullish.
i can explain, why, i know, i understand it etc. i see it...
ok, i buy... but the market goes against me a bit.. causing some temporal losses...

now, the emotions are tempting me to say "oh, fck it, i'm wrong, look my balance is negative, i want money not loss. etc.."
but then i must think about why i did it, and wait

comment.body:
so when you understand it, you can control your emotions.
and this saves you from chasing, fomoing into any direction.
this is key.

and a third thing, is sizing, but for the sake of this monologue, it's not the probabilistic aspect to maximize growth (see my post about Kelly).. but, that old rule that says never play with money that you are not ready to lose.
why is it important? - bcs the less emotional connection you have towards your money, the less temptation you have to deal with during red PnLs

comment.body:
sum: 
1) Control your emotions—don’t let your emotions control you. Time to grow up, no way around it.
2) Knowledge protects you. It gives you the strength to see through emotional storms (to know when to stop and when to push forward. Stay rational)
3) Only trade with money you’re ready to lose. (Cut the emotional attachment to money)
That’s why algos are better traders than humans. And that’s why the big players are always playing poker with the small ones, they have access to endless metrics.
Our job? Read between the lines, figure out where they’re headed, snatch a little cash from their pockets, and vanish.

Our job? Read between the lines, figure out where they’re headed, snatch a little cash from their pockets, and vanish.

comment.body:
"If this line breaks, 6045 will be magnetic to the downside with spot down/vol down. 
Now, considering that the zero vanna falls to this range and it can be broken with only lower vol, but also taking local negative color effect into account, during the AM there is more chance to break below the vanna flip than in the PM."
Market was squirrely today. Got broken with higher vol.

comment.body:
I don’t know, a lot of people seemed to understand today’s post..including me.
Alma specifically explained that if the market drops below 6105, the probability shifts toward further downside, with a break of 6085 confirming it. She pointed out that while gamma looks positive and should be supportive, in reality, this gamma exposure is set to decline over time, which increases volatility and pushes prices lower.

And when things are messy, we need to pay extra attention to key levels and what they actually mean. And she even posted that she bought puts..she said it back on Wednesday. You just cut the losses on calls, switch to puts, and that’s it. Simple.

comment.body: how do you see the IV?  how do i know when its weakening?
comment.raw_body: how do you see the IV?  how do i know when its weakening?

comment.body:
This has been discussed multiple times here and everywhere else, and Alma has explained it more than once:
VIX measures the volatility of ATM options with 30 days to expiration..always the ATM strikes, plus the wings with different weightings.
That’s why VIX naturally drops even when the market falls, because on the vol smile, if the strike shifts left (down), IV rises, and if it moves up, IV drops. Fixed-strike vol is what really matters, and from what Alma has written, that’s what she watches.
Just look at the term structure and observe how it moves. Every broker platform provides IV data..find it on yours.
Honestly, I don’t even understand how people trade without knowing these basics.
